untranslatable_reason: Signal requires advanced option Greeks (gamma notional γ²), bid/ask spread dynamics (ΔX), and volatility adjustment formulas based on position direction and rebalancing intervals. These require real-time options market microstructure data and volatility surface calculations not available in the standard indicator set. The core logic (σ_adjusted = σ₀√(1 ± 2γ²ΔX/wΔt)) cannot be expressed using price-based technical indicators alone. This is a quantitative vol/risk management rule requiring options pricing infrastructure, not a price-action backtest rule.
translation_notes: SINCLAIR_DRY_4_0 is a sophisticated volatility adjustment framework for option hedging. Entry condition triggers on initiating a short option position with measurable transaction costs and rebalancing intervals. Exit condition should trigger when realized volatility exceeds the theoretically-adjusted volatility threshold. However, without access to gamma exposure, bid/ask spreads, and option rebalancing frequency as data inputs, a meaningful backtest rule cannot be constructed. Recommend: (1) Extract realized vs implied vol divergence as a proxy, (2) Use hvol_20 as volatility proxy, or (3) Implement as a risk model overlay rather than a standalone trading signal.
